paper_title: Measurement of R-curve in clay brick blocks using optical measuring technique
sections_checked: 3.1. K_IC of clay brick blocks from R-curve
3.2. K_IC of clay brick blocks from CTOD
References checked for document completeness; not used as primary evidence

equations_checked.notes: K_I = FS/[B W^(3/2)] f(a/W); symbols and specimen dimensions verified against p.5.

The rendered formula is legible but displays a cube-root index on a/W and an unusual bracket structure. It was not transcribed into the evidence card and must be checked against the cited source before implementation.
Rendered as CTOD = K_I^2/(k sigma_ys E). The paper defines the correction coefficient and yield stress but does not report the numerical E or sigma_ys used.
CMOD/CTOD = a/[r(W-a)] + 1; r = 0.5 and central plastic-hinge assumption verified on p.6.

tables_checked.notes: All seven K_IC, CTOD_C and cross-method K_IC values were visually checked.

figures_checked.reason: Notch geometry is captured by dimensions in the parameter table.
General DIC apparatus photograph is replaceable by a short method description.
Sandwiched-beam setup is not needed for the current solver and is textually recoverable.
Pre-crack CMOD slope change is secondary to the final fracture evidence.
DIC crack-path thresholding is described in text; the image is not needed for cross-paper reasoning.
CTOD geometry is fully represented by Eq. (4) and its assumptions.
Irreplaceable view of seven specimen R-curves, scatter, gaps and validity boundary.
The two fitted groups are summarized numerically and Fig. 7 retains the underlying specimen evidence.
Shows the stable-to-unstable CMOD/load transition used by the CTOD method.

omitted_sections: Routine camera and lighting details not affecting retained conclusions
uncertain_items: Eq. (2) is visually legible but its rendered cube-root index and factorization are unusual; it was deliberately not copied as a solver-ready formula.
Numerical Young's modulus E and yield stress sigma_ys used in Eq. (3) are not reported in the paper.
The proposed link between porous heterogeneity and rapid crack propagation is an author conjecture requiring further confirmation.
known_warnings: R-curve values after a/W = 0.8 are declared unreliable by the authors.
Mode-I beam-scale values cannot be treated as local compression, shear, scratching or mixed-mode microspine thresholds.
BRK 2026 differs by 0.23 MPa sqrt(m) between the two K_IC methods.
DIC crack-tip tracking was manual and rapid propagation created unobserved intervals.
Fourth-order specimen fits are descriptive rather than constitutive.
